steps.title: Set the equations equal and factor
steps.content:
At intersection points, both expressions equal $y$:
$$4x^2 - 23x = x$$
Rearrange to standard form:
$$4x^2 - 24x = 0$$
Factor out $4x$:
$$4x(x - 6) = 0$$
This yields $x = 0$ or $x = 6$.

steps.title: Identify a from the non-zero solution
steps.content: We're given that one intersection is $(0, 0)$. The other intersection point is $(a, a)$, which corresponds to $x = 6$ from our factored solutions. Therefore, $a = 6$.
